ActionDiscriminatorRules.description: One if/then branch per live MCP action; constrains subaction requirement and the effective request DTO.

before.description:
Upper bound for temporal filter. Same formats as `since`.
Restricts results to content indexed on or before this date.

collection.description: Qdrant collection to search. Defaults to the server's configured collection.

diagnostics.description: Include RAG diagnostics in response. Overrides cfg.ask_diagnostics.

explain.description: Include per-candidate explain trace and skip LLM synthesis.

hybrid_search.description:
Per-request hybrid search override. `false` forces dense-only retrieval
(skips BM42 sparse + RRF). When unset, falls back to server config.

since.description:
Lower bound for temporal filter. Formats: 7d, 30d, YYYY-MM-DD, RFC3339.
Restricts results to content indexed on or after this date.

AxonToolResponse.description:
`AxonToolResponse` is the MCP `axon` tool's response envelope. Per the
tool contract (`docs/pipeline-unification/surfaces/tool-contract.md`,
§Design Rules: "Return structured envelopes for every response"), it is
converging toward the same shared envelope shape REST uses
(`axon_api::source::SuccessEnvelope`) — `request_id`/`contract_version`
are populated on every response; `job`/`watch`/`artifacts`/`pagination`/
`trace` are populated only where the constructing handler already has
that data (most call sites do not yet thread it through — see WS-G
followups), so they stay optional/empty rather than fabricated.

request_id.description:
Unique id for this response, generated once per call. Present on
every response (unlike the REST envelope's `request_id`, which is
assigned by transport middleware, this one is assigned here because
MCP has no equivalent per-request middleware seam yet).

AxonToolResponse.title: AxonToolResponse

render_mode.description:
Rendering engine override. Currently accepted but not applied — brand uses
a direct HTTP fetch and ignores the render_mode field. Reserved for a
future Chrome-backed extraction path.

CapabilitiesRequest.description:
`action=capabilities` — machine-readable runtime capability document
derived from the live `MCP_ACTION_SPECS` registry and provider doctor
data. See `docs/pipeline-unification/surfaces/tool-contract.md`
§Capabilities Action.
CapabilitiesRequest.title: CapabilitiesRequest

before.description:
Upper bound for temporal filter. Same formats as `since`.
Restricts results to content indexed on or before this date.

collection.description: Qdrant collection to search. Defaults to the server's configured collection.

diagnostics.description: Include RAG diagnostics in response. Overrides cfg.ask_diagnostics.

hybrid_search.description:
Per-request hybrid search override. `false` forces dense-only retrieval
(skips BM42 sparse + RRF). When unset, falls back to server config.

retrieval_ab.description: Compare hybrid RAG against dense-only RAG instead of RAG against baseline.

since.description:
Lower bound for temporal filter. Formats: 7d, 30d, YYYY-MM-DD, RFC3339.
Restricts results to content indexed on or after this date.

id.description:
`node`/`edge`: the id to look up. `resolve`: treated as the identifier
`value` (a stable key) when `node_id`/`canonical_uri` are unset.

include_edges.description: `node`: include incident edges in the response. Defaults to `false`.

include_evidence.description:
`node`: include edge evidence in the response. Edges already carry
evidence when loaded; this flag is accepted for contract parity and
currently has no additional effect beyond `include_edges`.

kind.description: `resolve`: hint for the identifier's expected node kind.

node_id.description: `query`: start node id (BFS root). Falls back to `id` when unset.

oneOf.description:
Job-cutover addition (2026-07-08): unified `embed`/`crawl`/`ingest`
enqueue paths in `axon-services`. Requires the `jobs.kind` CHECK
widened by `axon-jobs` migration `0021_job_kind_family_cutover.sql`.

archive_sources.description: `compact` — archive the source memories once merged.

export_scope.description: `export` — scope to export (all scopes when unset).

import_mode.description: `import` — how to reconcile with existing memories.

include_archived.description: `export` — include archived memories in the export.

include_working.description:
`export` — include `working`-status memories in the export (excluded
by default per contract "Type rules").

reason.description: Free-text reason recorded on `archive`/`forget`/`pin` history events.

MemoryRequest_MemoryDecayPolicy.description:
Explicit, inspectable decay configuration.

Contract: "Decay Contract" required fields.

MemoryRequest_MemoryHistoryEvent.description: Append-only history event recorded on every status/scoring change.

MemoryRequest_MemoryImportMode.description: How an import reconciles with existing memories for the same content.

oneOf.description:
Add records not already present (deduped by content hash/scope/type);
leave existing memories untouched.

Archive every existing memory in the target scope before importing.
Requires `axon:admin` at the transport boundary.

MemoryRequest_MemoryLink.description: Evidence-backed link from a memory to a source/entity/decision.

MemoryRequest_MemoryRecord.description: Full stored memory record. Contract: "Memory DTOs" — `MemoryRecord`.

contradicts.description: Memory this record conflicts with (`contradicted` status only).

superseded_by.description: Memory this record was replaced by (`superseded` status only).

visibility.description:
Security classification (contract "Security and Redaction": "classify
every memory by visibility"). Drives caller-scope filtering on
export/context (e.g. `sensitive` records excluded from export unless
the caller is authorized) independent of `MemoryStatus`.

MemoryRequest_MemoryScope.description:
Memory scope controlling recall and visibility.

Contract: "Scope Model" table (`global`, `project`, `repo`, `file`,
`source_id`, `graph_node_id`, `agent`, `user`, `environment`).

MemoryRequest_MemoryStatus.description:
Closed set of memory recall statuses.

Contract: "Memory Status" table — `active`, `review`, `superseded`,
`contradicted`, `archived`, `forgotten`, `working`.

oneOf.description: Eligible for recall.
Needs user/agent confirmation.
Replaced by another memory.
Conflicts with another memory and needs resolution.
Hidden from normal recall but retained.
Removed from recall and redacted/deleted according to policy.
Temporary memory with short TTL.

MemoryRequest_MemoryType.description:
Closed set of durable memory kinds.

Contract: "Memory Types" table — `decision`, `fact`, `preference`, `task`,
`bug`, `procedure`, `incident`, `entity`, `episode`, `working`.

oneOf.description: Durable design/implementation decision (slow decay).
Observed factual project/system state (normal decay).
User preference or standing instruction (very slow decay).
Work item or pending follow-up (normal decay).
Known defect or failure pattern (normal decay).
Repeatable operational procedure (slow decay).
Specific outage/failure/investigation (normal decay).
Stable entity profile such as repo/service/person/package (slow decay).
Session or event summary (fast decay).
Short-lived working context (very fast decay).

ProvidersRequest.description:
`action=providers` — provider capability/health discovery. `list`
(default) and `get` (requires `provider_id`) mirror the REST
`/v1/providers` resource-tier routes (`crates/axon-web/src/server/
handlers/providers.rs`), both backed by `axon_services::system::doctor`.
ProvidersRequest.title: ProvidersRequest

PruneMcpRequest.description:
`action=prune` — the target-state replacement for the legacy
`dedupe`/`purge` MCP actions
(`docs/pipeline-unification/surfaces/tool-contract.md` "Prune, Collections,
Graph, and Providers Actions").

`subaction` selects `plan` (dry-run, default-safe) or `exec` (destructive —
requires `axon:admin` and `confirm: true`). `target` is either a bare
source id or `collection:<name>` for a whole-collection prune, mirroring
the CLI's `axon prune plan|exec <target>` selector grammar.

confirm.description:
Required `Some(true)` for `subaction=exec` to proceed — mirrors the
CLI's `--confirm` gate and [`super::PurgeRequest::dry_run`]'s
`Option<bool>` idiom (a missing/`false` value is treated identically
by the handler; `Option` here is purely to match the doc-generator's
required/optional-field heuristic, not a wire-shape requirement).
Ignored for `subaction=plan`.

generation.description:
Narrow a source-id target to one generation. Invalid with a
`collection:` target.

subaction.description:
`plan` (dry-run) or `exec` (destructive). Defaults to `plan` when
omitted so a bare `prune` call never mutates state.

target.description:
Prune target: a bare source id, or `collection:<name>` for a
whole-collection prune. **Handler-required despite the `Option`** —
mirrors `PurgeRequest::target`'s "clean validation error, not a serde
rejection" convention.

PruneMcpRequest.title: PruneMcpRequest

before.description:
Upper bound for temporal filter. Same formats as `since`.
Restricts results to content indexed on or before this date.

collection.description: Qdrant collection to search. Defaults to the server's configured collection.

hybrid_search.description:
Per-request hybrid search override. `false` forces dense-only retrieval
(skips BM42 sparse + RRF). When unset, falls back to server config
(`AXON_HYBRID_SEARCH`, default true). Useful for A/B comparison.

since.description:
Lower bound for temporal filter. Formats: 7d, 30d, YYYY-MM-DD, RFC3339.
Restricts results to content indexed on or after this date.

ResolveRequest.description:
`action=resolve` — resolve source identity/adapter/route without acquiring
content. Backed by `axon_services::source::routing::resolve_source_route`.
ResolveRequest.title: ResolveRequest

before.description:
Upper bound for temporal filter. Same formats as `since`.
Restricts retrieved chunks to content indexed on or before this date.

collection.description: Qdrant collection to read from. Defaults to the server's configured collection.

cursor.description: Opaque cursor for fetching the next slice of document content.

since.description:
Lower bound for temporal filter. Formats: 7d, 30d, YYYY-MM-DD, RFC3339.
Restricts retrieved chunks to content indexed on or after this date.

token_budget.description: Maximum tokens to return in a single response slice. Default: 10,000.

SourceRequest.description:
Request parameters for the unified `source` action.

`source` is the single indexing entrypoint that replaces the removed
`embed` / `ingest` / `scrape` / `crawl` / `code_search` / `vertical_scrape`
MCP actions. The handler maps this onto [`crate::source::SourceRequest`] and
calls `axon_services::index_source`, which classifies the input (local path,
git URL, feed URL, youtube/reddit target, web URL, session selector, or
registry target), acquires it, and indexes it through the unified pipeline.

collection.description: Qdrant collection to index into. Defaults to the server's configured collection.

detached.description:
Run source indexing as a detached background `JobKind::Source` job
instead of blocking until it completes. When `true`, the response
carries a poll descriptor (`job_id`/`status`/`poll_after_ms`) instead
of the final `SourceResult`. Defaults to `false` (synchronous,
matching prior behavior).

scope.description:
Optional acquisition scope override (e.g. `page`, `site`, `repo`).
When omitted, the classified family's default scope is used.

source.description:
Source input string: a local path, git repository URL, feed URL,
youtube target, reddit target, web URL, session selector
(`session:<claude|codex|gemini>:<path>`), or registry target
(`pkg:<npm|pypi|crates>/<package>`). Accepts the `input` alias.

SourceRequest.title: SourceRequest

collection.description: Qdrant collection to inspect. Defaults to the server's configured collection.

limit.description: Maximum number of suggestions to return. Overrides cfg.search_limit.

exclude_selector.description: CSS selector to exclude elements before summarization.

render_mode.description: Rendering engine override (http | chrome | auto_switch). Overrides cfg.render_mode.

root_selector.description: CSS selector to scope content extraction before summarization.

collection.description:
Target collection for the source-request-backed watch store
(`subaction=update`/`subaction=create`). Unused by the legacy
create/list/get/exec/history subactions above.

embed.description:
Whether the source-request-backed watch (`subaction=create`) should
embed content on each run. Defaults to `false` when omitted.

source.description:
Source URI to watch (issue #298 WS-B `subaction=create`), e.g.
`https://example.com/docs` or `file:///path/to/repo`. Required for
`subaction=create`; unused otherwise.

oneOf.description:
Update a source-request-backed watch (issue #298 WS-B). Distinct
storage model from `create`/`list`/`get`/`exec`/`history` above — see
`crates/axon-jobs/src/watch_store.rs` module docs.
Disable a source-request-backed watch's scheduler execution.
Re-enable a source-request-backed watch's scheduler execution.
Hard-delete a source-request-backed watch and its run history.
